disabled who are in General Category or Other Backward 
Class Category as that process only would bring parity 
among all persons’ disparity irrespective of their vertical 
categories. This itself provides for justification to accord 
same concession, viz., 10% concession to PwDs as well, 
in all categories which is extended to those PwDs who fall 
in the category of SC/ST.
22. All the aforesaid clinchingly demonstrates that the 
people suffering from disabilities are equally socially 
backward, if not more, as those belonging to SC/ST 
categories and therefore, as per the Constitutional 
mandates, they are entitled to at least the same benefit 
of relaxation as given to SC/ST candidates.
52.1. This Court in Aryan Raj v. State (UT) of Chandigarh43 affirmed 
the above principle and held as follows:
